english: Comp. and deriv. γcíg-ka (gcig ka) single, only, opp. to several, Mil. - γcíg-c̉ar (gcig car), γcíg-čar (gcig char) v. c̉ar (car). - γcíg-c̉ig (gcig cig), pronounced *c̉ig-c̉ig* (*cig cig*), a certain, some one, εἷς τις, slób-ma-las γcíg-c̉ig (slob ma las gcig cig) Dzl.; bud-méd γcíg-c̉ig (bud med gcig cig) Dzl. VL, 5 (where Sch. has c̉ig-γcíg (cig gcig) erron.); γcíg-γcíg (gcig gcig), pronounced *c̉ig-c̉ig* (*cig cig*), 1. one at a time; separately, alone, esp. W.; 2. of the same kind, not different W. (v. Fouc. Gram. p. 21. 42), 3. adv. by one's self, only, solely W. - γcíg-c̉oγ (gcig chog) all-sufficient Glr. - γcíg-nyid (gcig nyid) Cs. 'unity' (?) - γcíg-tu (gcig tu) 1. into one, into one body, together, γcíg-tu sdú-ba (gcig tu sdu ba) to unite e.g. six countries, Dzl.; to contract, to simplify C. 2. at once, wholly, altogether Dzl. 22, 3; 3. firstly, in the first place, γcíg-tu-ni (gcig tu ni); then follows γnyís-su-ni (gnyis su ni) etc. Dzl. 4. only, solely Thgy. - γcíg-dú (gcig du) unity and plurality, γcíg-du-brál (gcig du bral) not having these two qualities Was. (308). γcíg-pa (gcig pa) 1. the first Wdn. (little used). 2. having etc. one, cf. dgu (dgu). 3. of one kind, not different or manifold, mí γcíg-pa (mi gcig pa) different B. and C. - γcíg-pu (gcig pu) (also ;c̉ig-bu? (cig bu?)) alone, single, γcíg-pus mi stóṅ ṭúb-pa (gcig pus mi stong thub pa) to be able to cope alone with a thousand men Dzl.; γcíg-pur lús-pa (gcig pur lus pa) to remain alone behind Glr.; only, sole, bu γcíg-pu (bu gcig pu) the only son, frq. - γcíg-po (gcig po) 1. alone, rgyál-po γcíg-po skyés-pa yin (rgyal po gcig po skyes pa yin) the king alone is a man, Dzl. 2. being one, or the one, ma γnyís-la skyés-pai bu γcíg-po (ma gnyis la skyes pai bu gcig po) thou (being the) one son of two mothers, viz. claimed by two, Glr. 3. Pur. the one - the other, - γcíg-šòs (gcig shos) the other, when speaking of two.
also γcì-ba (gci ba), pf. γcìs (gcis), fut. γcì (gci), imp. γcìs (gcis), to make water, to piss.

urine, γcìn γcìd-pa (gcin gcid pa), or γcì-ba (gci ba), W. tàṅ-c̉e (tang che), to make water; γcìn šor (gcin shor) urine is discharged involuntarily; γcìn-rkyág (gcin rkyag), both discharges, vulg.; γcìn-gág (gcin gag) the retention of urine Med.; γcìn-snyí (gcin snyi) gonorrhoea, clap(?) Med.
1. clyster-pipe = c̉eu (che'u); γcì-ui sman (gci 'ui sman) clyster Lex. - 2. clyster (?) Cs.
v. γcúd-pa (gcud pa); γcù (gcu) or lcù-kór (lcu kor) Cs., γcù-skór (gcu skor) W., γcùs-bu (gcus bu) Ts., screw. - γcù-ti (gcu ti) v. c̉u-ti (chu ti). - γcù-dóṅ (gcu dong) screw-box Cs.

prob. not different from γcágs-pa (gcags pa). The word occurs in: yíd(-la) γcùgs(-pa) (yid(-la) gcugs(-pa)) beloved, a friend Dzl.; mdza-γcùgs byéd-pa (mdza gcugs byed pa) to treat amicably Wdn.; kón-γcùgs (kon gcugs) having conceived a hatred Lex.
γcùd(-la) bór(-ba) (gcud(-la) bor(-ba)) Lexx. w.e.; Sch.: to forsake, to cast out, to reject; (cog. to c̉ud-záṅ? (chud zang?))

pf. γcùs (gcus) lcùs (lcus), fut. γcù (gcu), lcù (lcu), imp. γcùs(?) (gcus(?)) to turn, turn round, twist, twine, plait, braid; *c̉ud log táṅ-c̉e* (cud log tang che) W. to untwist, untwine a rope; *c̉us zum táṅ-te nol dug* (cus zum tang te nol dug) W. they wrestle and scuffle (prop. they fight scuffling); *sen c̉us gyáb-c̉e* (sen cus gyab che) W. to press and bore with the knuckle; *c̉ùs-spu* (*cus spu*) a low expression for the hair; lág-pa γcùs (lag pa gcus) Zam.?
secondary form of c̉ún-pa (chun pa), Lexx.: rtsád-nas γcùn-pa (rtsad nas gcun pa), prob. to subdue completely; γcùr-žiṅ γcùn-pa (gcur zhing gcun pa) prob. to beat or press a thing until it is soft.
secondary form of c̉úr-ba (chur ba); γcùr-pè (gcur pe) Ld. a coarse sort of vermicelli.
to esteem, to hold dear, to love Sch.
(Cs. γcèn-po (gcen po)) resp. Cs.: one's elder brother Dzl. MS, 11; acc. to Zam.: first-born son.

naked, col., also Mil.; γcèr-nyál (gcer nyal) id.; Kun.: *c̉er-góg* (*cer gog*); resp. sku γcèr-bu (sku gcer bu); γcèr-bu-rnams gábs-par byéd-pa yin-pa (gcer bu rnams gabs par byed pa yin pa) being one that covers the naked Stg.; γcèr-bur byín-pa (gcer bur byin pa) to make naked, to strip Pth.; γcèr-bu byúṅ-ba (gcer bu byung ba), W. *c̉er-nyál tón-c̉e* (*cer nyal ton che*) to show one's self naked; γcèr- (gcer-)
